topic.what: Anamorphic encryption under stronger adversarial settings where authorities perform network-level ciphertext sanitization
topic.how: Two-layer encryption combining NM-CPA inner layer for anamorphic protection with re-randomizable outer layer; deterministic AE for Interceptopia
topic.why_matters: Addresses realistic mass-surveillance threats where dictators deploy network nodes to re-randomize or decrypt-re-encrypt ciphertexts to eliminate covert communication channels

characteristics.what_it_measures: Size of ciphertexts in re-randomizable PKE instantiation
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.2: Cramer-Shoup Lite variant with reused randomness
characteristics.context: Black-box re-randomizable PKE construction outperforms prior work [20]

characteristics.vs_baseline: [20] required non-programmable random oracles; this work achieves plain model
characteristics.context: Plain model construction under DDH assumption

characteristics.vs_baseline: previous AE schemes vulnerable to re-randomization
characteristics.context: Scheme remains anamorphic even when ciphertexts are re-randomized by adversarial nodes
Significant efficiency gain while removing random oracle requirement

novelty: two-layer construction defeating Rerandtopia threat model
black-box re-randomizable PKE with 7 group elements under DDH in plain model
proof that strong re-randomization resistance implies normal re-randomization resistance (Theorem 1)
open_problems: efficient ARE (anamorphic-resistant encryption) without random oracles
extensions to chosen-ciphertext settings for deterministic AE
practical deployment considerations for the two-layer construction
trade-offs between ciphertext size and additional security properties
